entity.summary: Russian centralized cryptocurrency exchange launched in June 2017. By June 2019 the exchange had declared bankruptcy and arranged migration of users and remaining holdings to BITEXBOOK; the exact cessation date and primary cause remain uncertain.
entity.notes: The death date uses the first recovered public bankruptcy-and-migration documentation as a conservative terminal marker and may not be the exact final trading day. Secondary material mentions a security incident, but the evidence recovered for this batch is not strong enough to assign hack as the canonical death reason.

events.title: BitFlip began operating in Russia
events.description: BitFlip began operating in June 2017 as a Russian cryptocurrency exchange offering trading and exchange services.
events.notes: Month-level launch marker reconstructed from contemporaneous Russian reporting and the exchange's early public launch thread.

events.title: BitFlip bankruptcy and user migration were documented
events.description: Exchange-tracking records documented BitFlip's bankruptcy and the migration of users and remaining holdings to BITEXBOOK, marking the end of BitFlip as an independent exchange service.
events.notes: This is a public terminal-documentation marker rather than a guaranteed exact cessation date.

evidence.title: BitFlip presented the asset-backed FLIP coin
evidence.notes: Contemporaneous Russian reporting states that the exchange had operated since June 2017.

evidence.title: BitFlip cryptocurrency exchange launch thread
evidence.notes: Early public thread associated with the exchange's launch period and operating identity.

evidence.title: BitFlip exchange review and closure status
evidence.notes: The profile records a June 4, 2019 update describing declared bankruptcy and migration to BITEXBOOK.

evidence.title: BITEXBOOK exchange profile and BitFlip migration history
evidence.notes: Successor-platform profile corroborates the migration of BitFlip users and holdings to BITEXBOOK.
